title: Implement hidden slide placeholder generation
description: In thumbnails.py, add logic to detect hidden slides via python-pptx (slide.show == False) and replace their JPEG entry with a hatched grey placeholder image of the same dimensions, preserving slide index alignment throughout the list.
acceptance_criteria: Hidden slides are identified by reading the slide.show attribute via python-pptx
A hatched grey PIL Image is generated at the same pixel dimensions as a normal slide thumbnail
The returned image list has the same length as the total slide count
Hidden slide positions in the list contain the placeholder image, not a real render

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented `_make_hatched_placeholder(width, height) -> PIL.Image` — creates a light grey (200,200,200) background with diagonal hatch lines (160,160,160, spacing=20px). Implemented `generate_thumbnails(pptx_path, temp_dir) -> list[PIL.Image]` — reads slide visibility via python-pptx, delegates rendering to `pptx_to_jpegs`, detects whether LibreOffice included hidden slides, and returns a list of length == total slide count with placeholders at hidden positions. Added `TYPE_CHECKING` guard for `PILImage` annotation to avoid runtime import overhead.
handoff_summary.risks: LibreOffice behaviour with hidden slides is version/config-dependent; the dual-path mapping (total vs. visible JPEG count) handles both known cases, but a new edge case (e.g., partial rendering failure) would raise a clear RuntimeError. Callers consuming the returned PIL Images must close them after use to avoid file-handle leaks.
handoff_summary.next_action: Route to tester agent for acceptance-criteria validation.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected; only `pypptx/ops/thumbnails.py` was modified and no sibling beads claim this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used `slide.show is False` (identity check) so that `None` (attribute absent, meaning visible) is correctly treated as visible. Dual-path JPEG mapping handles both LibreOffice behaviours without requiring a LibreOffice config change. PIL imports are deferred inside functions to avoid import errors when Pillow is not installed and `check_dependencies` has not been called yet.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should cover: all-visible deck (list length == slide count, no placeholders), all-hidden deck (all placeholders), mixed deck with both LibreOffice modes (include-all and skip-hidden), and dimension consistency between placeholder and rendered images.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Placeholder dimensions fall back to 960×540 when the PPTX produces zero JPEGs (e.g., fully-hidden deck with LibreOffice skipping all slides). Callers must close returned PIL Images explicitly; the function does not manage image lifetimes.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected; only `pypptx/ops/thumbnails.py` was modified and no sibling beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Added `_make_hatched_placeholder` and `generate_thumbnails` to `pypptx/ops/thumbnails.py`. Hidden slides are detected via `slide.show is False` using python-pptx. The function handles two LibreOffice behaviours (includes or skips hidden slides in the PDF) by comparing the JPEG count against total vs. visible slide counts, then builds a full-length list inserting hatched grey PIL Images at hidden-slide positions.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
